q: I was a tax collector who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus. Who am I?
e: Luke 19:1-4 — Zacchaeus was a chief tax collector... he climbed a sycamore-fig tree to see him.

q: I was the first king of Israel. Who am I?
e: 1 Samuel 10:1 — Samuel took a flask of olive oil and poured it on Saul’s head.

q: I was known as the 'weeping prophet'. Who am I?
e: Jeremiah — Often called the weeping prophet due to his sorrow over Jerusalem.

q: I was a shepherd boy who defeated a giant named Goliath. Who am I?
e: 1 Samuel 17 — David killed the Philistine with a sling and a stone.

q: I was the mother of Samuel and prayed fervently for a son. Who am I?
e: 1 Samuel 1:20 — In the course of time Hannah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Samuel.

q: I was swallowed by a great fish after trying to flee from God's call to Nineveh. Who am I?
e: Jonah 1:17 — Now the Lord provided a huge fish to swallow Jonah.

q: I was the wisest king of Israel and built the Temple in Jerusalem. Who am I?
e: 1 Kings 3-6 — God gave Solomon wisdom... Solomon began to build the temple of the Lord.

q: I was a Moabitess who stayed with my mother-in-law Naomi. Who am I?
e: Ruth 1:16 — 'Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay.'

q: I was the cousin of Jesus and prepared the way for Him. Who am I?
e: Matthew 3:3 — 'A voice of one calling in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord.'

q: I was a queen who saved my people from Haman's plot. Who am I?
e: Esther 7-8 — Queen Esther pleaded for her people and Haman was executed.

q: I was the husband of Mary and a carpenter by trade. Who am I?
e: Matthew 1:16-19 — Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus.

q: I was the disciple who doubted Jesus' resurrection until I saw Him. Who am I?
e: John 20:25 — 'Unless I see the nail marks in his hands... I will not believe.'

q: I was the strongest man in the Bible, whose power came from my hair. Who am I?
e: Judges 16 — Samson's strength was linked to his Nazirite vow and his hair.

q: I was a Pharisee who came to Jesus at night to ask about being born again. Who am I?
e: John 3:1-3 — Now there was a Pharisee, a man named Nicodemus... he came to Jesus at night.

q: I was the brother of Mary and Martha, whom Jesus raised from the dead. Who am I?
e: John 11:43-44 — Jesus called in a loud voice, 'Lazarus, come out!'

q: I was a seller of purple cloth and the first convert in Philippi. Who am I?
e: Acts 16:14 — Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira.

q: I was the left-handed judge who killed King Eglon of Moab. Who am I?
e: Judges 3:15 — The Lord gave them a deliverer—Ehud, a left-handed man.

q: I was the prophet who succeeded Elijah and asked for a double portion of his spirit. Who am I?
e: 2 Kings 2:9 — 'Please let a double portion of your spirit be upon me,' Elisha said.

q: I was the high priest who presided over the trial of Jesus. Who am I?
e: Matthew 26:57 — Those who had arrested Jesus took him to Caiaphas the high priest.

q: I was the Roman governor who washed my hands of Jesus' death. Who am I?
e: Matthew 27:24 — Pilate took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd.

q: I was the son of Abraham and Sarah, born in their old age. Who am I?
e: Genesis 21:3 — Abraham gave the name Isaac to the son Sarah bore him.

q: I was the man who provided my own tomb for Jesus' burial. Who am I?
e: Matthew 27:57-60 — Joseph... placed it in his own new tomb.

q: I was the sister of Aaron and Moses, and a prophetess. Who am I?
e: Exodus 15:20 — Then Miriam the prophet, Aaron’s sister, took a timbrel.

q: I was the man who was forced to carry the cross for Jesus. Who am I?
e: Luke 23:26 — They seized Simon from Cyrene... and put the cross on him.

q: I was the wife of Isaac and mother of Esau and Jacob. Who am I?
e: Genesis 24-25 — Rebekah became Isaac's wife and bore twins.

q: I was the first martyr of the early church, stoned for my faith. Who am I?
e: Acts 7:59 — While they were stoning him, Stephen prayed.

q: I was a blind beggar healed by Jesus near Jericho. Who am I?
e: Mark 10:46 — A blind man, Bartimaeus... was sitting by the roadside begging.

q: I was the wife of King Ahab and famous for my wickedness. Who am I?
e: 1 Kings 16:31 — He married Jezebel daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians.

q: I was a centurion who was the first Gentile convert to Christianity. Who am I?
e: Acts 10 — Cornelius was a centurion... God told him to send for Peter.

q: I was the father of John the Baptist and was struck mute until his birth. Who am I?
e: Luke 1:13-20 — The angel said, 'Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son... you will be silent.'

q: I was the woman who had seven demons cast out of me and was the first to see the risen Jesus. Who am I?
e: Mark 16:9 — He appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.

q: I was the prophet who confronted King David about his sin with Bathsheba. Who am I?
e: 2 Samuel 12:7 — Then Nathan said to David, 'You are the man!'

q: I was the youngest son of Jesse and became the greatest king of Israel. Who am I?
e: 1 Samuel 16:11-13 — Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him.

q: I was a tentmaker who, along with my wife Priscilla, helped Paul. Who am I?
e: Acts 18:2-3 — Because he was a tentmaker as they were, he stayed and worked with them.

q: I was the king who saw the 'handwriting on the wall'. Who am I?
e: Daniel 5:1-5 — Suddenly the fingers of a human hand appeared and wrote on the plaster.

q: I was a judge who defeated the Midianites with only 300 men. Who am I?
e: Judges 7:7 — The Lord said to Gideon, 'With the three hundred men... I will save you.'

q: I was the man who survived the lions' den. Who am I?
e: Daniel 6 — God sent his angel, and he shut the mouths of the lions.

q: I was the disciple whom Jesus loved. Who am I?
e: John 21:20 — Peter turned and saw that the disciple whom Jesus loved was following them.

q: I was the son of David who led a rebellion against him and died when my hair got caught in a tree. Who am I?
e: 2 Samuel 18:9 — Absalom’s head got caught in the tree. He was left hanging in midair.

q: I was a prophetess and the only female judge mentioned in the Bible. Who am I?
e: Judges 4:4 — Now Deborah, a prophet, the wife of Lappidoth, was leading Israel.

q: I was the father-in-law of Jacob who deceived him into marrying Leah first. Who am I?
e: Genesis 29:23-25 — When evening came, he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob.

q: I was the high priest who trained the young boy Samuel. Who am I?
e: 1 Samuel 3:1 — The boy Samuel ministered before the Lord under Eli.

q: I was a man from the Land of Uz who remained faithful to God despite losing everything. Who am I?
e: Job 1:1 — In the land of Uz there lived a man whose name was Job.

q: I was the first person to see the burning bush. Who am I?
e: Exodus 3:2-3 — There the angel of the Lord appeared to him in flames of fire from within a bush.

q: I was a Roman centurion who said, 'Surely this man was the Son of God!' at the crucifixion. Who am I?
e: Mark 15:39 — When the centurion... saw how he died, he said, 'Surely this man was the Son of God!'

q: I was the wife of David and daughter of Saul who despised David for dancing. Who am I?
e: 2 Samuel 6:16 — She saw King David leaping and dancing... and she despised him in her heart.

q: I was the queen of Sheba who visited a king to test him with hard questions. Which king did I visit?
e: 1 Kings 10:1 — When the queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon... she came to test him.

q: I was the man who took over the role of apostle after Judas Iscariot. Who am I?
e: Acts 1:26 — Then they cast lots, and the lot fell to Matthias.

q: I was a colleague of Paul who was circumcised by him because of the Jews in the area. Who am I?
e: Acts 16:3 — Paul wanted to take him along... so he circumcised him because of the Jews.

q: I was the man who wrestled with an angel until daybreak. Who am I?
e: Genesis 32:24 — So Jacob was left alone, and a man wrestled with him till daybreak.
